game_name: The Great Circus Mystery Starring Mickey & Minnie

element_introduction_order.element: Water stream puzzle solving (fire, icicles, blocks)

element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Vertical traversal, item retrieval, enemy grab-throw

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Moderate difficulty with graduated outfit-based power progression; tension builds through enemy density and platforming complexity; relief provided by shops and health items strategically placed throughout levels

spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Hidden heart in first tileset of The Jungle level

difficulty_curve.early_game: Gentle introduction with basic jump/attack mechanics; Level 1 establishes core platforming
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Outfit acquisition (Levels 2-4) introduces specialized mechanics and resource management; grappling hook adds vertical complexity
notable_spikes: Level 2: Introduction of magic meter management alongside platforming
Level 3: Water spray mechanic combined with environmental puzzles
Level 4: Grappling hook vertical traversal challenges

notable_level_patterns.description: Each major level introduces a new outfit that becomes required/essential for progression through that level and subsequent challenges. Outfits can be upgraded in shops to increase effectiveness.
Outfit-based abilities consume meters (magic meter, water meter) that must be refilled by collecting specific items, creating resource management tension during enemy encounters and puzzle sequences.
The Jungle level uses identified tilesets (G22-02 for trees, G24 sets for boss area), suggesting thematic visual coherence across multiple jungle sections with distinct boss arena design.
Two-player mode uses alternating turn structure (confirmed for SNES version), suggesting level design accommodates single-character progression at a time rather than simultaneous action.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: All levels support both Mickey and Minnie via alternating play

sources_used: The Cutting Room Floor - The Great Circus Mystery (SNES)
